metadata.title: The Chain Rule Multiplies Local Ratios

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: A machine covered in thousands of dials slowly learns to stop being wrong. Each dial needs one number: how much does the total error change if I nudge this dial? The chain rule computes every number simultaneously by multiplying local ratios at each link.

card.copy: The Chain Rule Multiplies Local Ratios

beats.narration_text: A neural network has weights connecting layers. The error depends on the final output, which depends on every intermediate computation, which depends on every weight. Computing the full sensitivity seems to require tracing through everything.

production_viz.label: Neural network with weights connecting layers — error propagation path through all intermediate computations

beats.narration_text: Computing how much the error changes when one weight changes seems to require tracing through the entire network. How does the chain rule make this purely local?

card.copy: The error depends on every weight through a chain of computations. How does the chain rule avoid global lookup — and make each weight's sensitivity purely local?

beats.narration_text: Decompose the dependence. Weight w connects to computation z in the next layer. z connects to activation a via the activation function. a connects to the cost C. Three links in a chain.

production_viz.label: Three-link chain: w → z → a → C — each link with its local ratio labeled

beats.narration_text: The chain rule: dC over dw equals dz over dw, times da over dz, times dC over da. Each factor is a local ratio — computed only from information at that link. No link needs to know about the whole network.

production_viz.label: dC/dw = (dz/dw)(da/dz)(dC/da) — each factor local to its link, assembled as product

beats.narration_text: Concrete numbers: upstream activation a equals 0.7. So dz over dw equals 0.7. The sigmoid activation function has derivative 0.25 at typical values — da over dz equals 0.25. Cost gradient dC over da equals 2 times (output minus target) equals negative 0.4.

production_viz.label: Concrete values: dz/dw=0.7, da/dz=0.25 (sigmoid'), dC/da=-0.4 — each factor computed locally

beats.narration_text: Product: 0.7 times 0.25 times negative 0.4 equals negative 0.07. This weight's update is negative 0.07 times the learning rate — decrease it slightly. The weight was contributing too much to the error.

This generalizes to any depth. A network with 100 layers has 100 chain-rule factors per weight. Each factor is computed locally. Backpropagation assembles the product efficiently by accumulating from right to left, reusing previously computed terms.

production_viz.label: Right-to-left accumulation: backprop reusing saved products across 100-layer network depth

beats.narration_text: The key insight: the chain rule converts a global question — how does a change here affect something far away — into a product of local ratios. Each ratio answers a simpler question without knowing the rest of the network.

production_viz.label: Global → local conversion: chain rule transforms global sensitivity into product of local-only ratios

beats.narration_text: dC over dw equals (dz over dw)(da over dz)(dC over da). Each factor is local only. Example: 0.7 times 0.25 times negative 0.4 equals negative 0.07 — decrease this weight. Backpropagation accumulates from right to left, reusing saved products. Global sensitivity from local multiplication.

card.copy: dC/dw = (dz/dw)(da/dz)(dC/da). Each ratio is local. 0.7 × 0.25 × (-0.4) = -0.07. Backprop: accumulate right-to-left, reuse saved products. Global answer from local ratios.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
